summary: Full backend + frontend smoke test of AI Trading Engine SaaS (Flask+React). All 15 backend pytest cases PASS covering auth (register/login/me), signals, chart-candles, settings roundtrip, backtest + persisted runs/detail, journal CRUD, paper trade open/close, stats, equity. Frontend: login with trader@demo.com works, redirects to /dashboard which renders all 4 symbols (BTC/ETH/BNB/SOL) with Confidence panel; Backtester runs and shows trades/PnL; Settings page loads with Save button; Logout clears session and redirects to /login. Protected route guard verified (/api/auth/me without token = 401).

issues: Looks polished (fintech dark theme, green accent) - no issues observed

critical_code_review_comments: flask_app.py is ~1000 lines in a single module - consider splitting into blueprints (auth, market, backtest, journal, trades) for maintainability.
Signals endpoint iterates symbols sequentially fetching market data - could be parallelised with ThreadPoolExecutor to reduce first-call latency (observed ~5-15s).
SQLite with WAL is fine for demo but not suited for production concurrency - note for future migration.
JWT_SECRET has a default fallback value in code; ensure env var is set in production to avoid token forgery risk.
trades INSERT in open_paper_trade passes literal 'OPEN' inside the VALUES tuple as a placeholder value - works but mixing positional params with inline strings is brittle.
seed_data_creation: Test created one journal entry (auto-deleted) + one paper trade (closed) + one backtest run (persisted, harmless). Registered one throwaway user per run (TEST_<uuid>@demo.com).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test suite at /app/backend/tests/test_ai_trading.py is the regression baseline — 15 tests, ~30s runtime. Signals endpoint takes up to ~15s on first call due to live Binance/Coinbase fetch; use timeout>=60 for that test. Sidebar nav uses custom components, so playwright get_by_role('link') may not match every item; direct URL navigation (/backtester, /settings, /journal) is reliable.
rca of the issue: No bugs to RCA — all features behaving as specified.
